Document:
# PRECAUTIONARY CONFINEMENT Not criminal charges, used for protection of the colony.
## Mental Instability / Danger to Self or Others Held until medically cleared. Must be reviewed by medical staff or administration when possible (attempted suicide, severe delusions with violent behavior, attempting to breach exterior doors or tamper with life-support).
## Quarantine / Contamination Risk Isolated until cleared by medical or scientific authority. Escape from quarantine may become a capital crime if it endangers the colony. Public records should use general language (unknown pathogen, biological hazard, hostile lifeform, contamination risk) unless specific classifications have been formally released by lawful authority.
## Enemy Combatant / Prisoner of War Detained hostile combatant, pirate, terrorist, or armed attacker. Discretionary confinement, usually permanent until transferred or released by proper authority.
## Protective Custody Temporary hold for a person's own safety during active threats. Released once the threat passes. Not a criminal charge.
